name: Jabra Evolve2 65 Wireless Headset with USB-A Dongle

specs.Microphone: 6-mic array with beamforming technology for professional call quality
specs.ANC: Advanced ANC with multiple levels — light, medium, full
specs.Battery: 37 hours total (35 hours with ANC on)
specs.Certification: Microsoft Teams and Google Meet certified

whatWorks: The 6-microphone beamforming array is the core differentiator for business use — it focuses on your voice and suppresses background noise before the audio leaves the headset. Call participants consistently report hearing you clearly even when you're working in a noisy open-plan office or home environment.

The dual-device connectivity is practically useful: the USB-A dongle connects to your work PC while Bluetooth pairs to your phone simultaneously. You can switch between a Teams call on your computer and a mobile call without touching any pairing settings.

Battery life of 37 hours means a full work week on a single charge for most users — a Monday morning charge carries through Friday without worry. The USB-C charging cable and the option to use the headset while charging add additional flexibility.

Microsoft Teams certification goes beyond cosmetic branding — the Teams button on the headset launches Teams, answers and ends calls, and shows Teams call status via the LED indicator. For organizations standardized on Teams, this integration saves time across dozens of calls per week.
worthKnowing: The Evolve2 65 is a professional business headset, not a consumer audio headset. Music listening quality is decent but not the priority — Jabra optimizes for voice clarity, not bass or soundstage. If you want a headset that's equally good for music and calls, look at Sony or Bose.

At $299 CAD, this competes against the Poly Voyager Focus 2 and Logitech Zone Wireless. All three are excellent; the Evolve2 65 generally leads on call clarity and beamforming performance, while the Poly and Logitech options offer slightly more comfortable ear cushions for very long sessions.

The USB-A dongle is required for the primary low-latency wireless connection. Bluetooth is available as a secondary connection but introduces slightly more latency than the dongle. For calls, always use the dongle.

The flexible boom microphone arm folds up when not in use but is more visible than in-ear boom designs. In video calls, the arm is visible on camera — some users prefer a less conspicuous setup. The mic audio quality is noticeably better than any mic-embedded-in-headband design.

commonComplaints.title: Heavy for all-day wear…
commonComplaints.body: The over-ear design with the boom arm gets noticeable after 4-5 hours of continuous wear. Not painful but I take it off between calls to give my ears a break.

commonComplaints.title: Expensive compared to consumer options…
commonComplaints.body: You can get consumer wireless headsets with ANC for $100–150. The Evolve2 65 costs twice as much — justified by call clarity if your job is on calls all day, but hard to justify for occasional use.

commonComplaints.title: USB dongle occupies a port…
commonComplaints.body: The USB-A dongle is always plugged in and taking up a port. On a laptop with limited USB ports this is a real consideration, especially if you also use a USB-A wireless mouse.

commonComplaints.title: Teams app integration requires software…
commonComplaints.body: The Teams button requires the Jabra Direct software to be installed and running for full functionality. The headset works without it but you lose some Teams-specific features.

verdict: The Jabra Evolve2 65 is the benchmark professional business headset for call-heavy work environments. The 6-mic beamforming array delivers call clarity that makes a perceptible difference to everyone on the other end of your calls, and the 37-hour battery life with strong ANC makes it a real all-day work tool. At $299 it's a professional investment, not a casual purchase, but for anyone whose job involves sustained phone and video conferencing it earns back its cost quickly in reduced call friction.

realBuyers.quote: My team kept asking me if I'd upgraded my microphone. I hadn't — I just switched to the Evolve2 65. The difference in call quality is immediately audible to everyone on the call, not just me.
Full work week on one charge, ANC blocks my open-plan office completely, Teams button works perfectly. This is the headset I should have bought when I started remote work.
Switching between my laptop dongle and my phone via Bluetooth is seamless. Answer a Teams call on the computer then take a mobile call without any re-pairing. Genuinely useful.
I'm on calls 6 hours a day. This headset has held up to that schedule for 18 months without any issues. Worth every dollar for the reliability alone.

metaDescription: Jabra Evolve2 65 wireless headset review for Canada: 6-mic beamforming, 37-hour battery, Teams certified, dual-device. Best business headset. ~$299 CAD.
introText: The Jabra Evolve2 65 (ASIN B08TPBK6RQ) is a professional wireless business headset with a 6-microphone beamforming array, 37-hour battery, and Microsoft Teams certification, available on Amazon.ca at around $299 CAD.
